audits.excerpt:
Only then did the northward pull in Jaina's head clear.

No crowd of roads around it now. No ward-stones. No city smoke. One line rose out of the horizon, harder than before.
audits.reason: The departure threshold still shifts into abstract signal-language instead of staying with ship, horizon, and bodily carrier. The ache arrives, but the prose explains the cleared line in concept terms rather than making the new openness physically legible.
audits.suggestedFix: Only then did the ache behind her teeth sharpen. With the harbor gone behind them, the pressure stopped smearing sideways through road and stone and drew straight over the bow into the dark.

audits.excerpt: The line north had been clean at the harbor. Out here it thickened into resistance. Pressure layered on pressure. Deliberate.
audits.reason: This is still theory-language. The scene needs the resistance to cash out in bow, wind, and shove, not in diagnostic abstraction about what the line is doing.
audits.suggestedFix: At the harbor the pull had run straight. Out here it bunched in front of the bow and shoved back each time the schooner tried to nose north.

audits.excerpt: Jaina found herself respecting Dagny against inclination.
audits.reason: The prose names the shift instead of letting Dagny's mast-hand, order timing, and complaint-sorting earn it. That sentence sits above the deck rather than inside Jaina's observed seamanship read.
audits.suggestedFix: Delete this sentence and let the following ship-handling details carry the shift without naming it.

audits.excerpt: Yesterday she might have bristled at it. Now it only sounded exact.
audits.reason: The dawn exchange slips into polished emotional summary just when cracked skin, frost, and deck fatigue should keep the beat embodied. The realization is valid, but this phrasing explains it from outside the body.
audits.suggestedFix: Jaina rubbed cracked salt from her thumb joint and let the answer stand.

audits.excerpt: every sound aboard suddenly more deliberate because careless wood on hidden ice would kill them more efficiently than any haunted wind had managed.
audits.reason: "Haunted wind" re-poeticizes the crossing at the exact moment the approach should narrow into pole-work, hull risk, and ice geometry. The line overstates the supernatural register instead of keeping the danger nautical and immediate.
audits.suggestedFix: every sound aboard suddenly more deliberate because careless wood on hidden ice would stove the hull faster than the headwind had.

audits.excerpt: Jaina read the timing from the wood, the burned hull, the emptied camp edge, and the crust over old boot marks.
audits.reason: This summarizes a deduction instead of grounding it in actionable age-signs. The evidence is present, but the prose still reports the conclusion from above rather than making the weathering thresholds and boot wear do the work.
audits.suggestedFix: The new dock cuts were still pale, but the boot ruts at the camp edge had frozen, thawed, and frozen again under a skin of drift.

audits.excerpt: Another continent. Another stage of the pursuit. Too late for comfort. Still time to keep after Arthas.
audits.reason: The close collapses into outline-thinking and thesis language. After a strong sensory stack, the chapter should finish in dock, lungs, and inland drag rather than summarizing the next story function.
audits.suggestedFix: Ice. Char. Old camp grease. The inland pull caught at her before the deck-roll had left her knees.
